reviews.review_description: This game is kinda short but definitely sweet,  I had a wonderful time playing and  going back for Seconds. A very good Narrated adventure horror story game, the only thing I'd like improved is that you can turn the TV on throughout the game, not just at the beginning of the game. But the rest of the game is really fun and good, definitely Recommended.

developer_response.body: Hello! We are delighted as well that you loved the game. What part did you find the shortest? We are always open to improve

reviews.review_description:
What on earth is this game???? The game launches, .I see the table with a tv but I can’t go towards it, an invisible step is blocking it. I can’t go straight, I can’t go right.. I go back and I fall in a void, everything is pitch black…that’s it . I didn’t die but what on earth???! 

how do I play??

UPD: so I wanted to give it a chance esp after developers’ response and repurchased the game. Yes they did fix that bug. But. The game is such a disappointment… waste of money. To start off it’s extremely short with no logical ending. There are two locations and two puzzles..umm and thats it.. you can’t interact with most objects, you can’t do tasks out of order which makes no sense. It’s very boring and kinda dumb. There’s a bug where if you take off the headset , once yo put it back you can’t sit anymore  and if you were sitting you can’t stand up anymore. You have to quit and start over. So far this is the worst game I have ever played in VR.

Mandela Invasion VR: Alternates is a chillingly unique VR experience where you play as an agent investigating a house where an entire family vanished. It starts slow and eerie—chilling, eating, watching TV—before ramping up the tension with driving scenes, puzzles, and an incredibly intense atmosphere.
The house interior is stunningly detailed, and the subtle back-and-forth events keep you on edge, wondering what new horror awaits. At just an hour long (early access), it’s tightly paced with a solid story that unfolds naturally.
If you love supernatural mysteries with a slow-burn horror vibe, this is a must-try. Just don’t expect jump scares—this one gets under your skin in the best way. 🕯️🏚️
